questionText: Você está apresentando os resultados trimestrais para a diretoria e quer introduzir um ponto positivo inesperado. Qual expressão é mais adequada?
correctAnswer: What's particularly encouraging is that...
options: What's particularly encouraging is that...
I am happy to tell you about...
Something good happened, which is...
Also, there is a nice thing...

questionText: Você precisa recusar educadamente um pedido do seu chefe sem parecer confrontador. Qual é a resposta mais profissional?
correctAnswer: I appreciate you thinking of me, but I'm afraid my current workload wouldn't allow me to give this the attention it deserves.
options: I appreciate you thinking of me, but I'm afraid my current workload wouldn't allow me to give this the attention it deserves.
Sorry, I'm too busy to do that right now.
No, I don't think I can handle that.
I don't have time for this project.

questionText: Durante uma reunião, você quer pedir esclarecimentos sobre um ponto sem parecer que não prestou atenção. O que dizer?
correctAnswer: Could you elaborate a little further on that point?
options: Could you elaborate a little further on that point?
What do you mean? I didn't understand.
Can you say that again?
I didn't get what you said.

questionText: Você quer agradecer a um colega por sua ajuda em um projeto difícil de forma genuína e profissional. O que dizer?
correctAnswer: I genuinely couldn't have pulled this off without your support.
options: I genuinely couldn't have pulled this off without your support.
Thanks for your help with the project.
You helped me a lot, thank you.
Good job on helping me.

questionText: Você está em uma entrevista de emprego e quer demonstrar interesse genuíno pela empresa. Qual pergunta é mais adequada ao nível C2?
correctAnswer: What does success look like in this role over the first six months?
options: What does success look like in this role over the first six months?
Do I get a good salary here?
When can I get a promotion?
Is the work hard?

questionText: Você quer propor uma ideia nova em uma reunião sem parecer impositivo. Qual expressão é mais adequada?
correctAnswer: I'd like to throw an idea out there if I may.
options: I'd like to throw an idea out there if I may.
Listen to my idea now.
I have an idea and you should hear it.
Let me tell you what to do.

questionText: Você precisa encerrar uma ligação profissional de forma educada e decisiva. O que dizer?
correctAnswer: I think we've covered everything we needed to. I'll follow up with a summary by email.
options: I think we've covered everything we needed to. I'll follow up with a summary by email.
OK, bye now.
I have to go. Talk later.
We're done here.

questionText: Você quer expressar concordância com uma análise de mercado apresentada por um colega em uma reunião executiva. O que dizer?
correctAnswer: That aligns perfectly with what we've been observing in the field.
options: That aligns perfectly with what we've been observing in the field.
Yes, I agree with you.
Same thing I was thinking.
You are right about that.

questionText: Você precisa delegar uma tarefa a um subordinado de forma clara e respeitosa. Qual é a abordagem mais profissional?
correctAnswer: I'd like to hand this over to you — I trust your judgment on how best to approach it.
options: I'd like to hand this over to you — I trust your judgment on how best to approach it.
Do this task for me, please.
You need to work on this now.
Here, take this and do it.

questionText: Você está em uma negociação e quer sinalizar que está aberto a concessões. Qual é a expressão mais adequada?
correctAnswer: There may be some room for flexibility on our end.
options: There may be some room for flexibility on our end.
We can change things if you want.
OK, we can lower our price.
We are open to many things.

questionText: Você quer avisar um cliente de um possível atraso na entrega sem criar alarme desnecessário. O que dizer?
correctAnswer: I wanted to give you a heads-up that we may be facing a slight delay, though we're doing everything to stay on schedule.
options: I wanted to give you a heads-up that we may be facing a slight delay, though we're doing everything to stay on schedule.
We will be late with your order.
There is a problem with your delivery.
Sorry, we cannot deliver on time.

questionText: Você quer parabenizar um colega por uma promoção de forma sincera e natural. O que dizer?
correctAnswer: You've more than earned this — congratulations!
options: You've more than earned this — congratulations!
Good for you, you got promoted.
Congratulations on your new job title.
Nice, you got a promotion.

questionText: Você quer verificar se todos entenderam as instruções após uma apresentação. O que dizer ao final?
correctAnswer: Does that all make sense, or would anyone like me to clarify anything?
options: Does that all make sense, or would anyone like me to clarify anything?
Did you understand everything I said?
Any questions? Tell me if not.
Is everything clear now?

questionText: Você precisa solicitar um prazo maior para entregar um relatório ao seu gerente. O que dizer?
correctAnswer: Would it be possible to have a couple more days? I want to make sure the report is as thorough as possible.
options: Would it be possible to have a couple more days? I want to make sure the report is as thorough as possible.
Can I have more time? I'm not done yet.
I need more days to finish the report.
Give me a deadline extension, please.

questionText: Você precisa apresentar-se profissionalmente em um evento de networking. Qual é a introdução mais eficaz?
correctAnswer: I lead the digital transformation initiatives at a mid-sized fintech — always keen to connect with people working at the intersection of tech and finance.
options: I lead the digital transformation initiatives at a mid-sized fintech — always keen to connect with people working at the intersection of tech and finance.
Hi, I work in technology at a company.
My name is X and I work in finance and tech.
I am a manager at a fintech business.

questionText: Você quer interromper gentilmente alguém que está falando durante uma reunião para adicionar um ponto importante. O que dizer?
correctAnswer: Sorry to jump in, but I think this is relevant to what you're saying.
options: Sorry to jump in, but I think this is relevant to what you're saying.
Stop, I want to talk now.
Wait, let me speak first.
Excuse me, I am talking.

questionText: Você quer mencionar em uma entrevista que superou metas anteriores sem soar arrogante. O que dizer?
correctAnswer: In my previous role, I was fortunate enough to consistently exceed the targets set for the team.
options: In my previous role, I was fortunate enough to consistently exceed the targets set for the team.
I always did better than everyone else at my old job.
I was the best employee they ever had.
My results were the highest in the company.

questionText: Durante uma reunião de crise, você quer expressar que a situação é séria mas controlável. Qual expressão é mais adequada para um executivo C2?
correctAnswer: The situation is challenging, but it's well within our capacity to manage if we act decisively.
options: The situation is challenging, but it's well within our capacity to manage if we act decisively.
This is bad, but we can fix it if we try hard.
Things are not good, but we can handle it.
It's a big problem, but don't worry too much.

questionText: Você está negociando um contrato e quer indicar que chegou ao limite das suas concessões sem romper as negociações. O que dizer?
correctAnswer: I have to be candid — we've reached the outer limits of what I'm authorised to offer at this stage.
options: I have to be candid — we've reached the outer limits of what I'm authorised to offer at this stage.
I can't give more, that's the max.
We don't have room to lower the price anymore.
This is our final price, take it or leave it.

questionText: Você quer dar feedback construtivo a um colega sobre um relatório mal estruturado sem ser ofensivo. O que dizer?
correctAnswer: The content is solid, but I wonder if restructuring the argument might make it land more effectively with the audience.
options: The content is solid, but I wonder if restructuring the argument might make it land more effectively with the audience.
This report is not very good, you should rewrite it.
The report has problems with its structure.
You need to change how the report is written.

questionText: Você está conduzindo uma entrevista de emprego e quer avaliar a capacidade analítica do candidato. Qual pergunta é mais eficaz?
correctAnswer: Walk me through how you'd approach diagnosing a significant drop in customer retention.
options: Walk me through how you'd approach diagnosing a significant drop in customer retention.
How do you solve difficult problems?
Are you good at analysis and thinking?
Tell me about a time you solved something hard.

questionText: Você quer persuadir seu conselho de administração a aprovar um investimento de alto risco. Como introduzir o argumento?
correctAnswer: The case for this investment rests on three pillars, each of which significantly de-risks the proposition.
options: The case for this investment rests on three pillars, each of which significantly de-risks the proposition.
This investment is risky but we should do it because of three reasons.
I think we should invest because there are good reasons for it.
Let me tell you why this is a good investment despite the risks.

questionText: Você quer expressar discordância com uma decisão estratégica durante uma reunião executiva sem minar a autoridade do líder. O que dizer?
correctAnswer: I respect the direction we're heading, though I'd feel remiss if I didn't flag a potential blind spot.
options: I respect the direction we're heading, though I'd feel remiss if I didn't flag a potential blind spot.
I don't agree with this decision and I want to say why.
I think you are wrong about this strategy.
This plan has a problem that we are not seeing.

questionText: Em uma negociação salarial, você quer ancorar o valor de sua remuneração com base em pesquisa de mercado. O que dizer?
correctAnswer: Based on my research into current market benchmarks for this level of seniority, I'd be looking at a range in the region of...
options: Based on my research into current market benchmarks for this level of seniority, I'd be looking at a range in the region of...
I know what people like me earn, so I want more money.
The market pays more for my experience, so I need a higher salary.
I want a salary that matches what others get in similar jobs.

questionText: Você quer encerrar uma reunião improdutiva que está se prolongando sem solução à vista. O que dizer diplomaticamente?
correctAnswer: I think we've surfaced all the key tensions here. Perhaps we'd benefit from letting this percolate and reconvening with fresh perspectives.
options: I think we've surfaced all the key tensions here. Perhaps we'd benefit from letting this percolate and reconvening with fresh perspectives.
This meeting is taking too long, let's stop and think more later.
We are going in circles. Let's end the meeting.
I think we should stop because we are not making progress.

questionText: Você precisa comunicar uma demissão inesperada a sua equipe de forma profissional e empática. Como iniciar o comunicado?
correctAnswer: I want to share some news with you directly and with the transparency you deserve.
options: I want to share some news with you directly and with the transparency you deserve.
I have something to tell everyone, which is not good news.
So, someone from our team is leaving and I need to tell you.
There is some bad news that you all need to hear.

questionText: Você quer demonstrar liderança durante uma situação de conflito entre equipes na empresa. Qual postura comunicativa é mais eficaz?
correctAnswer: Let's take a step back from our respective positions and focus on what outcome serves the organisation best.
options: Let's take a step back from our respective positions and focus on what outcome serves the organisation best.
Both teams need to stop arguing and think about the company.
You are all fighting but you should think about what is better for work.
Stop the conflict and focus on your jobs.

questionText: Você quer recomendar um ex-colega para uma posição de liderança de forma convincente por escrito. Qual abertura é mais adequada?
correctAnswer: It is without reservation that I recommend [Name] for this position — they are quite simply one of the most effective leaders I have had the privilege of working alongside.
options: It is without reservation that I recommend [Name] for this position — they are quite simply one of the most effective leaders I have had the privilege of working alongside.
I think [Name] would be a great choice for this leadership role.
[Name] is a very good leader and I recommend them.
This person should get the job because they lead well.

questionText: Você está fazendo uma apresentação de vendas e quer tratar a objeção de preço de um cliente sem ceder imediatamente. O que dizer?
correctAnswer: I understand price is a consideration. Let me walk you through the return on investment so you can weigh up the full picture.
options: I understand price is a consideration. Let me walk you through the return on investment so you can weigh up the full picture.
Yes, it is expensive, but it is worth it.
The price is high but you should still buy it.
Many clients think it's expensive but then they buy it anyway.

questionText: Você precisa mobilizar sua equipe após uma série de fracassos no projeto, mantendo o moral elevado. O que dizer?
correctAnswer: Every setback we've faced has sharpened our understanding — we're now better positioned than ever to deliver.
options: Every setback we've faced has sharpened our understanding — we're now better positioned than ever to deliver.
We failed many times but let's try again and succeed.
Things went wrong, but I think we can do better now.
Don't be sad about the failures, let's work harder.

questionText: Você está conduzindo uma revisão de desempenho e quer abordar a falta de iniciativa de um funcionário de forma construtiva. O que dizer?
correctAnswer: One area I'd like to explore with you is how you might take greater ownership of identifying opportunities rather than waiting for direction.
options: One area I'd like to explore with you is how you might take greater ownership of identifying opportunities rather than waiting for direction.
You need to be more proactive and take more initiative at work.
I want you to stop waiting for people to tell you what to do.
You should try to find opportunities yourself instead of waiting.

questionText: Você quer comunicar uma mudança organizacional importante aos funcionários minimizando a resistência. Qual abordagem comunicativa é mais eficaz?
correctAnswer: I want to acknowledge that change of this magnitude is rarely comfortable, and I commit to keeping you informed every step of the way.
options: I want to acknowledge that change of this magnitude is rarely comfortable, and I commit to keeping you informed every step of the way.
There will be big changes but don't worry, everything will be fine.
Changes are happening and I will tell you what you need to know.
This is a big change so I understand if you are nervous, but it will be OK.

questionText: Você quer questionar diplomaticamente as premissas de uma proposta estratégica durante uma apresentação ao conselho. O que dizer?
correctAnswer: I wonder whether the assumptions underpinning this projection have been stress-tested against a more adverse scenario.
options: I wonder whether the assumptions underpinning this projection have been stress-tested against a more adverse scenario.
Have you thought about what happens if things don't go as planned?
What if the predictions in this plan are wrong?
Are you sure about the numbers in this proposal?

questionText: Você está mediando um conflito entre dois gerentes e quer redirecionar a conversa para soluções. Qual intervenção é mais eficaz?
correctAnswer: I hear both perspectives clearly. What would be most productive now is to channel that energy into mapping out a path forward.
options: I hear both perspectives clearly. What would be most productive now is to channel that energy into mapping out a path forward.
You both have good points, now let's think about how to fix this.
OK, I understand what you both are saying. Let's find a solution now.
Both of you are right in different ways. Let's solve the problem.

questionText: Você precisa convencer um conselho relutante a aprovar um orçamento de inovação sem precedentes históricos na empresa. Qual abertura retórica é mais persuasiva?
correctAnswer: The very absence of precedent here is the point — disruption, by definition, requires us to chart territory we haven't navigated before.
options: The very absence of precedent here is the point — disruption, by definition, requires us to chart territory we haven't navigated before.
We haven't done this before, but that's why it's innovative and we should try.
Just because it's new doesn't mean it's bad, so let's approve the budget.
Innovation means doing things for the first time, so we should invest.

questionText: Durante uma fusão corporativa delicada, você precisa responder à imprensa sem confirmar nem negar rumores. Qual resposta é mais adequada?
correctAnswer: As a matter of policy, we don't comment on market speculation, but I can assure you that any material developments will be communicated through the appropriate channels.
options: As a matter of policy, we don't comment on market speculation, but I can assure you that any material developments will be communicated through the appropriate channels.
We cannot talk about this topic because it is confidential.
There is nothing to say about the rumours at this time.
I am not allowed to answer questions about possible mergers.

questionText: Você quer criticar a cultura de microgestão de um executivo sênior em uma reunião de liderança sem causar constrangimento direto. Como fazê-lo?
correctAnswer: One pattern I've been reflecting on across the organisation is whether our oversight mechanisms might inadvertently be signalling a lack of trust in our people.
options: One pattern I've been reflecting on across the organisation is whether our oversight mechanisms might inadvertently be signalling a lack of trust in our people.
Some managers are controlling too much and this makes employees feel not trusted.
I think certain leaders need to give their teams more freedom and trust.
Micromanagement is a problem that is hurting morale in the company.

questionText: Você está fechando um acordo de parceria e percebe que a outra parte omitiu informações relevantes. Como abordá-los diplomaticamente?
correctAnswer: There are a few areas where our due diligence has surfaced discrepancies that I'd appreciate the opportunity to walk through together before we proceed.
options: There are a few areas where our due diligence has surfaced discrepancies that I'd appreciate the opportunity to walk through together before we proceed.
We found some things that you didn't tell us and we need to talk about them.
Our research shows that you hid some information from us.
Before we sign, I want to discuss some things you didn't mention.

questionText: Você precisa rejeitar uma proposta de um parceiro estratégico de longa data sem comprometer o relacionamento futuro. Qual resposta demonstra maior sofisticação comunicativa?
correctAnswer: While the proposal is compelling in several respects, the timing and strategic fit don't align with where we need to be right now — though I'm genuinely hopeful this conversation opens a longer-term dialogue.
options: While the proposal is compelling in several respects, the timing and strategic fit don't align with where we need to be right now — though I'm genuinely hopeful this conversation opens a longer-term dialogue.
We can't accept your proposal now, but maybe in the future we can work together.
Your offer is good, but we are not interested at this moment.
We are rejecting the proposal, but we value our partnership.

questionText: Você está liderando uma sessão de estratégia e quer desafiar o pensamento de grupo sem atacar nenhuma ideia individualmente. Qual técnica comunicativa é mais eficaz?
correctAnswer: Let me play devil's advocate here — if this strategy were to fail spectacularly, what would have been the root cause?
options: Let me play devil's advocate here — if this strategy were to fail spectacularly, what would have been the root cause?
I don't think this strategy is good because there are possible problems.
What if this plan doesn't work? Let's think about the reasons why.
We should think more carefully because the strategy might fail.

questionText: Você é CEO e precisa comunicar resultados abaixo das expectativas a investidores sem causar pânico, mas sendo completamente honesto. Qual abertura é mais adequada?
correctAnswer: I want to be unambiguous: this quarter fell short of what we set out to deliver, and I intend to give you a clear-eyed account of why and what we're doing about it.
options: I want to be unambiguous: this quarter fell short of what we set out to deliver, and I intend to give you a clear-eyed account of why and what we're doing about it.
Our results this quarter were not good, but we have a plan to improve.
We didn't achieve our goals this quarter, but please don't worry too much.
The numbers are below expectations, but we will do better next time.

questionText: Você quer influenciar uma mudança cultural profunda na organização sem usar mandato ou autoridade hierárquica. Qual abordagem comunicativa é mais sofisticada?
correctAnswer: Culture shifts when enough people start behaving differently — my goal is to make the case so compellingly that the change becomes self-sustaining.
options: Culture shifts when enough people start behaving differently — my goal is to make the case so compellingly that the change becomes self-sustaining.
I want to change the culture but I can't force people, so I need to convince them.
We need cultural change and I will try to persuade people to act differently.
Cultural transformation requires changing how employees think and behave.

questionText: Durante uma negociação multilateral complexa, você percebe que duas partes formaram uma aliança contra você. Como abordar isso estrategicamente?
correctAnswer: I've noticed a convergence of positions that, if it continues, may create an impasse for all of us. Perhaps it's worth exploring what a trilateral arrangement might look like.
options: I've noticed a convergence of positions that, if it continues, may create an impasse for all of us. Perhaps it's worth exploring what a trilateral arrangement might look like.
I can see that you two are working together against me, which is not fair.
It looks like both of you have the same position, which is a problem for me.
You seem to be aligned, so maybe we should find something that works for everyone.

questionText: Você é headhunter e quer abordar um executivo de alto nível que não está ativamente procurando emprego. Qual abordagem inicial é mais eficaz?
correctAnswer: I'm not reaching out because I assume you're looking — I'm reaching out because the opportunity is specific enough that I'd be remiss not to bring it to your attention.
options: I'm not reaching out because I assume you're looking — I'm reaching out because the opportunity is specific enough that I'd be remiss not to bring it to your attention.
I know you are not looking for a job, but I have a good opportunity for you.
Even though you are not in the job market, I found a position you might like.
I have an offer that could interest you, even if you are not looking.

questionText: Você precisa reposicionar uma falha estratégica passada como aprendizado organizacional durante um discurso aos acionistas. Qual enquadramento é mais sofisticado?
correctAnswer: That chapter, while costly, fundamentally recalibrated our risk appetite and directly informed the discipline that underpins our current trajectory.
options: That chapter, while costly, fundamentally recalibrated our risk appetite and directly informed the discipline that underpins our current trajectory.
We made a mistake in the past, but it taught us important lessons that we use today.
The failure was expensive, but it made us more careful and that's why we do well now.
Although we failed before, we learned from it and now we are doing better.

questionText: Você está conduzindo uma due diligence verbal e quer extrair informações sensíveis de um potencial parceiro sem parecer intrusivo. Qual abordagem é mais eficaz?
correctAnswer: I'd love to understand how you've navigated the regulatory landscape in your core markets — that tends to be where the real operational complexity lives.
options: I'd love to understand how you've navigated the regulatory landscape in your core markets — that tends to be where the real operational complexity lives.
Can you tell me about the regulatory issues you have had in your markets?
What problems have you faced with regulations in the countries where you operate?
I need to know how you handle legal and regulatory compliance in your business.

questionText: Você precisa encerrar o contrato de um fornecedor estratégico de longa data por razões de performance, preservando a relação para possível retomada futura. Como comunicar?
correctAnswer: This hasn't been an easy decision to arrive at. Our assessment is that the partnership, as currently structured, isn't delivering what either of us needs — and I believe we both deserve better than that.
options: This hasn't been an easy decision to arrive at. Our assessment is that the partnership, as currently structured, isn't delivering what either of us needs — and I believe we both deserve better than that.
We have decided to end the contract because you are not meeting our requirements.
Unfortunately, your performance has not been good enough to continue the partnership.
We need to end the contract, but we hope to work together again in the future.

questionText: Você é advogado corporativo e precisa alertar um cliente sobre riscos legais sérios em uma proposta de negócio sem desestimulá-lo completamente. Qual abordagem é mais adequada?
correctAnswer: The commercial rationale is sound, but there are several exposure points that, if left unaddressed, could substantially undermine the value proposition — let me walk you through the mitigation options.
options: The commercial rationale is sound, but there are several exposure points that, if left unaddressed, could substantially undermine the value proposition — let me walk you through the mitigation options.
The business idea is good, but there are legal risks that could be a big problem.
I like the business plan, but the legal issues could cause serious problems for you.
This could be a good deal, but you need to be careful about the legal risks involved.

questionText: Você está em uma conferência internacional e precisa articular a posição competitiva da sua empresa em relação a concorrentes maiores. Qual enquadramento é mais estrategicamente sofisticado?
correctAnswer: Our scale may be asymmetric, but our agility and depth of client relationships consistently compensate in ways that don't show up in market share figures.
options: Our scale may be asymmetric, but our agility and depth of client relationships consistently compensate in ways that don't show up in market share figures.
We are smaller than our competitors, but we are faster and our clients trust us more.
Even though we are not as big as them, we have advantages in flexibility and relationships.
Our company is smaller, but we compete well because of our speed and good service.

questionText: Você precisa mediar um impasse entre dois departamentos que têm visões completamente opostas sobre prioridades orçamentárias. Como reencadrar a discussão?
correctAnswer: Rather than arbitrating between two competing claims, I'd like to reframe this as a joint exercise in mapping resource allocation to strategic outcomes.
options: Rather than arbitrating between two competing claims, I'd like to reframe this as a joint exercise in mapping resource allocation to strategic outcomes.
Instead of choosing between the two departments, let's think about what helps the company most.
Both departments have valid points, so let's focus on what the strategy requires.
Let's not fight about the budget and instead think about what we need to achieve.
